scenario: Helix Healthcare orchestrates a triage workflow that creates 1200 instances per day on Camunda 8.8. A Node.js worker needs to continuously claim and complete CREATED user tasks via the Tasklist API client, polling once every 1000 milliseconds rather than running a one-shot search of the queue.
question: Which configuration turns camunda.searchUserTasks into a continuous user task subscription in the Camunda 8.8 TypeScript SDK?

options.text: Set the consistency option pollIntervalMs to zero so the SDK polls the Tasklist API as fast as the network allows without blocking the loop
Set the consistency option waitUpToMs to Infinity so the SDK keeps polling and treats the predicate function as a long-running subscription
Pass a webhookUrl alongside the filter so the Tasklist gateway pushes every newly created user task to the SDK over an HTTP callback channel
Set the option streaming to true on the search request so the gateway opens a server-sent events channel and pushes user tasks to the client

a.text: Incorrect. Setting pollIntervalMs to zero is not the documented subscription switch; the SDK turns searchUserTasks into a subscription when waitUpToMs is set to Infinity.

b.text: Correct. The user task poller comment states that setting waitUpToMs to Infinity converts the predicate into a polling subscription that continues to run until cancelled.

c.text: Incorrect. The user task example uses polling via searchUserTasks; there is no webhookUrl option documented for Tasklist callbacks in the SDK example.

d.text: Incorrect. The SDK example uses polling with the consistency options pollIntervalMs and waitUpToMs, not a server-sent events streaming flag on the search request.

explanation: The programmatic user task worker uses camunda.searchUserTasks with consistency.waitUpToMs set to Infinity and a predicate function that claims and completes each task; this configuration is what turns the search into a continuous subscription.
